design_goal: Intake runtime that preserves shared clinical guardrails but makes budgeting and question scheduling complaint-specific and authoritative.

supported_operators.description: Operators used in ask_if and skip_if logic blocks. All operators are evaluated by the runtime engine.

operators.description: Always evaluates to true.
Checks if a field equals a given value.
Checks if a field does not equal a given value.
Checks if a field is greater than or equal to a numeric value.
Checks if a field is below a numeric threshold.
Checks if a field exists and has a non‑null value.
Checks if a text field contains a substring.
Checks if a text field contains any of the given substrings.
Checks if a duration field (e.g., 'duration') is at least a given value with unit.
Checks if a duration field is below a given threshold with unit.
Checks if a field is present in the patient's known diagnoses (PMH).
Checks if a clinician has set an exclusion flag for a system.
Checks if a field has already been asked in a co‑active overlap session.
Checks if the current escalation level is at least the given level.
Checks if the patient gave a clear negative for an entire ROS system.
Checks if an entity was positively extracted from narrative.
Checks if a field exists and its numeric value is below a threshold.
Checks if a parent boolean field equals a value (used for child detail skipping).
Logical OR of multiple conditions.
Logical AND of multiple conditions.

session_guardrails.emergent_override_rule: A newly volunteered red-flag symptom may temporarily pre-empt the scheduled complaint flow without creating a third planned complaint.

authoritative_runtime_principles.runtime_source_of_truth: Complaint wording lives in complaint.questions_by_id. Shared registries define response types, gating, deduplication, module ownership, overlap resolution, and escalation behavior.
authoritative_runtime_principles.complaint_budget_authority: Every complaint owns its own budget. There is no universal complaint-level target budget shared across all symptoms.
authoritative_runtime_principles.schedule_authority: Each complaint's question_schedule is executable order, not a hint. The engine must not infer order from question arrays.
authoritative_runtime_principles.pathological_system_ros_principle: After complaint clerking, finish the active complaint's pathological-system ROS first, then use remaining ROS budget on selected cross-system concepts without repeating already covered concepts.
authoritative_runtime_principles.history_module_principle: PMH/PSH, social/family, medication/allergy, gynecologic, and immunization history remain dedicated modules and should not be duplicated in complaint files unless explicitly marked as acute symptom-critical exceptions.
authoritative_runtime_principles.cross_file_overlap_principle: When active complaints overlap, the higher-acuity complaint owns shared danger concepts first. The second complaint may only ask unresolved concepts not already captured, negated, or derived.
authoritative_runtime_principles.phase_interleave_principle: Safety-sensitive complaints should front-load early danger screens before lower-yield descriptive questions.
authoritative_runtime_principles.entity_extraction_fallback: If entity extraction is uncertain, ask rather than skip. Missing a critical question is worse than mild redundancy.
authoritative_runtime_principles.concept_resolution_principle: A concept should be asked once per session. Parent/detail trees and ROS must skip concepts already resolved in complaint clerking, derived fields, or prior ROS.

NARRATIVE_FREE_TEXT.description: Open narrative answer in the patient's own words.
examples: It started yesterday and has been getting worse.

BOOLEAN_WITH_OPTIONAL_DETAILS.description: Usually yes/no, optionally followed by details if positive.

TEMPORAL_OR_SHORT_TEXT.description: Short description of timing, chronology, or course.
TEMPORAL_OR_SHORT_TEXT.allowed_values: Short text or normalized duration/time value.

COUNT_OR_SHORT_TEXT.description: Numeric count when available, otherwise a short description.

NUMERIC_OR_SHORT_TEXT.description: Numeric value when measurable, otherwise short descriptive text.
NUMERIC_OR_SHORT_TEXT.allowed_values: Number, number with unit, or short text.

TEMPORAL_WITH_UNIT_OR_SHORT_TEXT.description: Time-based answer that should preferentially capture explicit units such as minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, or years; short text permitted when approximate.
TEMPORAL_WITH_UNIT_OR_SHORT_TEXT.allowed_values: Short text or normalized temporal value with unit.

ALWAYS.description: Ask this question in every encounter for the complaint.

SEX_FEMALE_REPRODUCTIVE_AGE.description: Ask only if the patient is female and plausibly of reproductive age.

SEX_FEMALE.description: Ask only if the patient is female.

PAIN_SITE_IS_BREAST.description: Ask only when pain site or lump site is breast.

PAIN_SITE_IS_FLANK.description: Ask only when pain site is flank.

PAIN_DISTRIBUTION_MULTISITE.description: Ask when pain distribution indicates multiple sites.

PAIN_UNEXPLAINED_OR_HIGH_ACUITY.description: Ask when pain is high severity or has systemic features.

PAIN_TIMING_UNRESOLVED.description: Ask timing question only if the timing pattern has not been resolved by a danger-screen question (e.g., severe_constant_pain).

MUCOSAL_INVOLVEMENT_POSITIVE.description: Ask only if mucosal involvement has been confirmed.

AURA_STATUS_POSITIVE.description: Ask only if aura status is positive.

PATIENT_PEDIATRIC_OR_CAREGIVER_HISTORY_R.description: Ask if patient is paediatric or caregiver is present.

SUSPECTED_INFECTIOUS_PRESENTATION_OR_RES.description: Ask when infection is suspected or presentation is respiratory.

ELDERLY_OR_HIGH_RISK_RESPIRATORY_PRESENT.description: Ask for elderly patients or those with respiratory infection signs.

DURATION_GTE_4_WEEKS_AND_AGE_GTE_65.description: Ask when symptom duration >= 4 weeks AND patient age >= 65.

AGE_GTE_50_OR_VASCULAR_RISK.description: Ask if patient age >= 50 or has known vascular risk factors.

VASCULAR_RISK_PRESENT.description: Ask if patient has known vascular risk factors.

SEXUAL_HISTORY_RELEVANT_SESSION.description: Ask when complaint could relate to STI.

INFECTIOUS_OR_GU_PRESENTATION.description: Ask when infection or genitourinary symptoms make recent antibiotic use relevant.

POSSIBLE_TB_PRESENTATION.description: Ask when TB is part of the differential.

POSSIBLE_CNS_INFECTION_PRESENTATION.description: Ask when meningitis or another CNS infection is possible.

GYNECOLOGIC_CONTEXT_RELEVANT.description: Ask when the encounter involves a gynecologic, urogenital, lower abdominal, vomiting, or pregnancy-related context.

SEXUALLY_ACTIVE.description: Ask when the patient is sexually active or when sexual activity is plausibly relevant.

FRESH_BLOOD_STOOL_RELEVANT.description: Ask only if fresh red blood still needs clarification apart from melena.

CHANGE_IN_BOWEL_HABIT_POSITIVE.description: Ask only if bowel habit change was confirmed.

FIELD_ALREADY_CAPTURED.description: Skip if the canonical field already has a normalized answer in state.

DETAIL_ALREADY_CAPTURED.description: Skip if the detail field for this question is already populated.

PARENT_NEGATIVE.description: Skip child detail questions when their parent finding is explicitly negative.

SEVERITY_BELOW_8.description: Skip extreme pain screens if severity < 8.

TIMING_ALREADY_RESOLVED.description: Skip timing question if the timing pattern has already been determined by a danger-screen question (e.g., severe_constant_pain).

COMPLAINT_IS_TRAUMATIC.description: Skip gradual cause questions when trauma is positive.

ACUTE_RED_FLAG_ACTIVE_SKIP_CHRONIC_SCREE.description: Skip long-term screening when an immediate red flag is active. This condition requires engine support for escalation level tracking.
ACUTE_RED_FLAG_ACTIVE_SKIP_CHRONIC_SCREE.applies_when: any derived_red_flag_pattern is active at immediate_alert or urgent_escalation tier

SYMPTOM_VOLUNTEERED_IN_NARRATIVE.description: Skip if symptom already mentioned in opening narrative. Requires entity extraction support.

NEGATIVE_SYSTEM_SCREEN_SKIP_DETAILS.description: Skip all targeted_ros questions for a system if patient gave clear negative for that system. Requires system screen tracking.

SAME_FIELD_ASKED_IN_OVERLAP_SESSION.description: Skip if field already asked in co-active overlap session. Requires session overlap state tracking.

PAIN_SITE_NOT_FLANK.description: Skip flank disambiguation if pain site is not flank.

PATIENT_PEDIATRIC.description: Skip adult-only questions for children under 12.

COMPLAINT_ALREADY_EXCLUDED_BY_EXAM.description: Skip if clinician exam has ruled out the system. Requires clinician override input.

KNOWN_DIAGNOSIS_EXPLAINS_FINDING.description: Skip if finding is explained by known chronic diagnosis. Requires PMH data.

DURATION_BELOW_2_WEEKS.description: Skip chronicity-dependent screens when duration <2 weeks.

ROS_CONCEPT_ALREADY_COVERED.description: Skip ROS question if concept already captured elsewhere.

DURATION_BELOW_4_WEEKS.description: Skip chronic-only questions if duration <4 weeks.

actions: Do not let patient leave waiting area unaccompanied

urgent_escalation.description: High acuity — clinician must assess within minutes.
actions: Priority queue the session for clinician review

priority_clinician_review.description: Concerning — clinician should review before discharge.
actions: Ensure summary is reviewed before patient leaves

same_day_clinician_review.description: Warrants review today but not immediately life-threatening.
same_day_clinician_review.response_time: Within the same session or before patient leaves.

documentation_guidance: Use yes/no when the question is binary.
If yes, preserve any clinically useful qualifier in a paired detail field or note.
Use unknown when the patient cannot say; use not_assessed when the question was not asked.

principles: Explain briefly why the question matters clinically before asking when appropriate.
Use neutral, non-judgmental language and avoid assumptions.
Ask in private when possible, especially for sexual or reproductive history.
Allow the patient to decline or defer if they are uncomfortable.
Record the answer factually without moral or diagnostic language.
sensitive_questioning_policy.default_intro: I ask this routinely because it can change what may be causing the symptoms and what tests or treatment are safest.
sensitive_questioning_policy.private_setting_note: If another person is present, confirm the patient is comfortable answering before continuing.

ui_rendering_rules.use: display_name for complaints and ui_label or text for questions
never_show: raw field ids with underscores or internal routing codes
ui_rendering_rules.note: Raw ids may remain in the backend for stability, but the UI should render only readable labels.

fever.meaning: Patient reports subjective or measured fever.

duration.meaning: How long the symptom has lasted.

character.meaning: Quality of the symptom (e.g., sharp, dull, burning).

chest_pain.meaning: Presence of chest pain or tightness.

palpitations.meaning: Awareness of heart beating (fast, irregular, pounding).

fatigue.meaning: General tiredness or lack of energy.

pregnancy_context.meaning: Whether patient could be pregnant or is postpartum.

immunocompromised_status.meaning: Weakened immune system due to disease or treatment.

pain_router_to_site_specific_child.rule: pain.json owns site and exact location only. Once a dominant site is identified, the child complaint owns characterization, red flags, and complaint-specific ROS. The child complaint must not re-ask generic pain-site questions already answered.

cardiopulmonary_overlap.rule: Ask the highest-acuity cardiopulmonary complaint first. Shared danger concepts such as chest pain, breathlessness, syncope, palpitations, hemoptysis, and exertional triggers must be deduplicated across all active cardiopulmonary complaints.

event_overlap.rule: loss_of_consciousness owns fainting, blackout, and collapse presentations. seizure owns witnessed convulsive, tonic-clonic, or clearly epileptic presentations. Do not repeat full event characterization across both routes; unresolved features should be handed off, not re-asked from scratch.

urinary_overlap.rule: dysuria owns burning, urgency, and painful voiding features. hematuria owns blood-characterization features. Shared questions such as fever, flank pain, obstruction, and pregnancy context must be asked once and reused.

gastrointestinal_overlap.rule: The dominant GI complaint owns broad characterization. Secondary GI complaints may ask only unresolved danger features or a limited secondary budget subset. Shared dehydration, bleeding, and obstruction concepts must not be repeated.

final_closeout_question.text: Is there anything else about your symptoms, recent health, or circumstances that you think is important and that we have not already asked about?

selection_guidance.source_basis: Use the curated ROS systems and question sets from the codebook summary template and Hutchison direct-questions framework.

single_complaint.primary_system_questions: exhaust unanswered high-yield concepts first within budget
single_complaint.secondary_cross_system_questions: then complete other systems in priority order while staying within the same session budget
single_complaint.default_total_ros_questions: 3-5 if low risk; 4-6 if one red flag is active

multiple_complaints.primary_and_secondary_pathological_syste: Exhaust unanswered high-yield concepts for the primary complaint, then the permitted secondary complaint, before lower-yield cross-system ROS.
multiple_complaints.default_total_ros_questions: Usually 6-10 total ROS questions across the session, with override only when safety concerns remain unresolved.

selection_guidance.do_not_repeat_rule: If a symptom clerking question already captured the concept directly or by derived equivalent, keep the ROS item available in the bank but skip it for that session.
evidence_basis: Hutchison direct questions about bodily systems not covered by the presenting complaint
User codebook ROS sections and summary template system headings
selection_guidance.non_repeat_rule: If a symptom question, derived field, shared history item, or previously rendered ROS question already covered the concept, do not render it again in the session.
selection_guidance.session_cap_note: ROS shares the same session cap and must not become a second full interview.

ros_priority_algorithm.description: Defines how the engine uses the 'clinical_priority' field (high/medium/low) to select ROS questions when the remaining ROS budget is limited.

steps.action: Determine the active complaint's targeted_ros_plan. Identify the primary pathological system, high_yield_systems, and cross_system_systems (if any).
Calculate the remaining ROS budget from the complaint's ros_mode (target_ros_questions - already asked ROS questions). If remaining budget <= 0, stop.

For the primary pathological system, collect all unanswered ROS questions (from ros_question_bank.json) that belong to that system and are not already covered by the complaint's own questions or earlier answers.
Sort those questions by clinical_priority: high first, then medium, then low. Within the same priority, no further ordering is required (engine may use original order).

Ask questions in that sorted order, one by one, decrementing the remaining budget after each question, until either the budget is exhausted or no more unanswered questions remain in the primary system.
If budget remains, repeat steps 3-5 for each high_yield_system (in the order listed in the complaint's targeted_ros_plan).

If budget still remains, repeat steps 3-5 for cross_system_systems, but only ask questions with clinical_priority = high (unless the complaint's ros_mode.max_ros_questions is not yet reached and the engine has discretion to ask medium priority cross-system questions – this is implementation dependent).
If budget remains after exhausting all systems, do not ask further ROS questions (the budget is intentionally conservative).

edge_cases.condition: A red flag pattern is active (e.g., immediate_alert or urgent_escalation).
edge_cases.action: The engine may temporarily override priority ordering and ask all high-priority questions from the pathological system first, but should still respect the remaining budget. It may also escalate to ask additional ROS questions beyond the target budget if safety requires (up to max_ros_questions).

edge_cases.condition: A question in ros_question_bank.json has no clinical_priority field.
The complaint's ros_mode.max_ros_questions is set to a value lower than the number of high-priority questions in the primary system.
edge_cases.action: Ask only the first N high-priority questions (where N = remaining budget). Do not ask medium or low priority questions from any system if the budget is exhausted by high-priority questions alone.

ros_priority_algorithm.implementation_note: This algorithm must be implemented in the runtime engine. The JSON files only provide the data (clinical_priority) and this rule set. The engine does not need to read the algorithm from JSON; it is provided here as documentation for developers.
